user_message: Summarize what happened with Tailspin Toys and give me the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-10 08:12 EST
Subject: QBR preparation - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-17 11:59 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with QBR preparation. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-19 15:52 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about QBR preparation. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Tailspin Toys
Date: 2024-01-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), Kevin (Tailspin Toys)

[00:01:30] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into QBR preparation.

[00:05:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around stakeholder alignment.

[00:10:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:14:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:17:00] Steven Wilson (Tailspin Toys): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about technical validation. Can we address those?

[00:22:00] Kevin (Tailspin Toys): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-L trap (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-L failure mode (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-L -- Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
